Title: TaDL interacts with TaB3 and TaNF‐YB1 to synergistically regulate the starch synthesis and grain quality in bread wheat

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major storage carbohydrate determining grain filling, grain quality, and processing/eating quality
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in developing grains, peaking in developing grains; very low in roots, stems, and leaves
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in developing grains, peaking in developing grains; very low in roots, stems, and leaves
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates TaSUS2 and TaAGPL2 transcription; positively associated with expression of TaSUS1, TaSUS2, TaAGPL2, TaSBEIIa, TaGBSSII, and TaSWEET2a
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation via direct promoter binding; synergistic activation in complex with TaB3 and TaNF-YB1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive regulator of starch accumulation and grain quality in wheat; knockout reduces starch, amylose, amylopectin, grain width, and 1,000-grain weight
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a complex with TaB3 and TaNF-YB1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes starch accumulation, amylose/amylopectin accumulation, grain filling, grain width, and grain weight; improves starch physicochemical properties
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaDL is a grain-preferential nuclear YABBY TF in wheat. 2. CRISPR tadl mutants showed reduced grain width, 1,000-grain weight, starch, amylose, and amylopectin, while TaDL overexpression increased grain width and grain weight. 3. TaDL directly binds the promoters of TaSUS2 and TaAGPL2 and activates their transcription. 4. TaDL physically interacts with TaB3 and TaNF-YB1, and the three factors synergistically enhance activation of starch biosynthetic genes. 5. A natural TaDL-4B elite allele (HapI/BI) is associated with higher starch content and higher TaDL transcript level.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; overexpression; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; subcellular localization; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; yeast two-hybrid; co-immunoprecipitation; pull-down; luciferase complementation imaging; BiFC; haplotype association analysis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing grains sampled mainly at 8, 10, 15, and 20 DAF under greenhouse or field growth conditions
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Triple knockout caused barren plants; knockout reduced grain size/weight and altered starch pasting and gelatinization properties; pleiotropic effects on awn elongation reported from previous study
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for overexpression/transient assays; native promoters of TaSUS2 and TaAGPL2 in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for improving wheat starch content and grain quality; TaDL-B elite allele HapI/BI and STARP1 marker are useful for marker-assisted selection; overexpression or favorable allele deployment may enhance grain filling, but severe knockout causes strong penalties
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TaDL-B HapI/HapII variation lies in the 3′ UTR region and may affect mRNA abundance; triple mutant sterility/barrenness prevented full analysis of all-homeolog knockout

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major storage carbohydrate determining grain filling, grain quality, and processing/eating quality
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: B3 family transcription factor with B3 DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positively contributes to TaSUS2 and TaAGPL2 expression; directly binds RY motifs in TaSUS2 and TaAGPL2 promoters; alone shows limited activation but enhances TaDL-mediated activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: DNA binding to RY motifs and cooperative transcriptional activation with TaDL and TaNF-YB1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator required for normal starch accumulation; knockout phenocopies tadl with reduced starch and poorer starch properties
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with TaDL and participates in TaDL-TaB3-TaNF-YB1 complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes starch accumulation, grain width, grain weight, and normal starch granule size and viscosity properties
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaB3 is a developing-grain-expressed nuclear B3 TF. 2. tab3 triple mutants exhibited reduced grain width, 1,000-grain weight, total starch, amylose, amylopectin, starch granule size, and viscosity. 3. TaB3 physically interacts with TaDL and is part of a TaDL-TaB3-TaNF-YB1 complex. 4. TaB3 binds RY motifs in TaSUS2 and TaAGPL2 promoters and enhances TaDL-dependent transcriptional activation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; RT-qPCR; subcellular localization; co-immunoprecipitation; pull-down; luciferase complementation imaging; BiFC; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; starch and grain phenotype analysis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing grains; phenotypes evaluated in field-grown plants and developing grains at grain-filling stages
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout reduces grain weight, starch accumulation, starch granule size, and starch viscosity traits
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate positive regulator for engineering enhanced starch accumulation, potentially useful in regulator stacking with TaDL and TaNF-YB1
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although TaB3 can bind target promoters, its transcriptional activation alone is limited; its major effect appears cooperative with TaDL and TaNF-YB1

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major storage carbohydrate determining grain filling, grain quality, and processing/eating quality
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Overexpression increases transcript levels of TaSUS2, TaAGPL2, and TaSWEET2a; enhances TaDL-mediated activation of TaSUS2 and TaAGPL2; mutants show decreased TaSUS2 and TaAGPL2 expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: cooperative transcriptional activation with TaDL and TaB3; likely promoter-associated regulation through NF-Y recognition context
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator of grain filling and starch accumulation; overexpression increases starch content, while knockout reduces grain size and starch granule size
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with TaDL and participates in TaDL-TaB3-TaNF-YB1 complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes starch accumulation, grain width, grain weight, endosperm cell size, and starch granule size
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNF-YB1 positively regulates wheat grain filling and starch accumulation. 2. taNF-YB1 mutants have thinner grain cross sections, smaller endosperm cells, and smaller A-type starch granules. 3. TaNF-YB1 overexpression increases grain width, 1,000-grain weight, total starch content, and expression of TaSUS2, TaAGPL2, and TaSWEET2a. 4. TaNF-YB1 physically interacts with TaDL and enhances TaDL/TaB3-dependent activation of starch biosynthetic genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Previously reported CRISPR/Cas9 knockout referenced in this paper; overexpression; RT-qPCR; immunoblotting; paraffin sectioning; SEM; co-immunoprecipitation; pull-down; luciferase complementation imaging; BiFC; dual-luciferase assay
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing grains at 15, 20, and 35 DAF under field conditions
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising positive regulator for improving starch content and grain filling; may be effective in combinatorial engineering with TaDL and TaB3
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In this paper, TaNF-YB1 functions mainly as a cooperative regulator rather than a strongly autonomous activator of TaSUS2/TaAGPL2
